failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: terminal_reward component assigned high positive reward (failure_mean=10.0) to early_failure trajectories, encouraging exploitation of survival rather than safe landing.
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: Replaced terminal_reward with safe_landing_bonus and crash_penalty components to reward only safe landings (both legs contact, low speed, small angle) and penalize crashes.
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: Candidate shows high failure avoidance (0.988), but no success_like preference pairs exist (decision_level=no_decision) due to low success_like count (21/360) in training set.

lesson: Terminal_reward must be restructured to avoid rewarding crashes, but without sufficient success_like trajectories, success depends on making safe landing conditions stringent enough to convert partial_progress into success_like behavior; component_recomposition can help but requires careful threshold tuning.
future_guidance: Preserve approach_progress, proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus, low_progress_timeout components.
Ensure safe_landing_bonus conditions are stringent to avoid rewarding non-landing terminations.
Avoid global survival bonus without progress gating.
Consider recalibrating approach_progress to better discriminate partial_progress from early_failure.
Monitor training to verify that survival exploitation decreases and safe landing rate increases.

archive_update.reason: Selected candidate not yet evaluated for long training; elite archive best is round3_candidate1_progress_conditioned with 10 success_like trajectories.

uncertainties: Low success_like count (21/360) relative to partial_progress (316/360); no preference pairs possible.
Decision level remains no_decision; cannot build strong selection signals.
Terminal_reward anomaly may be fixed in candidate, but training outcome not yet confirmed.
Episode length saturation (mean ~999/1000) in parent may distort true progress measures.
